title: Add embed deps, build.rs guard, and decouple sqlite data_dir from static_dir
action: Add `rust-embed = { version = "8", features = ["debug-embed"] }` and `axum-embed = "0.1"` to Cargo.toml dependencies (per D b300856d). Create build.rs at the crate root that does `std::fs::create_dir_all("static").expect(...)` and prints `cargo:rerun-if-changed=static`, so `cargo build`/`test`/`clippy` never fail when `web/` hasn't been bundled yet (static/ is gitignored). In src/config/mod.rs add `pub fn data_dir() -> PathBuf` mirroring the existing `config_dir()` exactly but joining `${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/herdr-gateway` instead of the config base — this must resolve to the exact same path install.sh's current default PREFIX ($HOME/.local) already produces for SHARE_DIR, so existing sqlite state is found unchanged on upgrade. In src/main.rs, replace the sqlite path derivation currently at the `config.static_dir.parent().unwrap_or(...).join("herdctl-state.sqlite")` call with `herdctl::config::data_dir().join("herdctl-state.sqlite")`, creating the data dir first if missing (`std::fs::create_dir_all`). Do not change static_dir's own meaning/type in Config yet — that is embed-pkg-2's job.

truths: `data_dir()` on a default environment (no XDG_DATA_HOME set) returns `$HOME/.local/share/herdr-gateway`, matching install.sh's current default SHARE_DIR exactly
A fresh checkout with no static/ directory present still compiles and passes `cargo test`/`cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
Non-demo runs create the sqlite db under data_dir(), not under static_dir's parent

artifacts.substantive: creates static/ if missing before the crate's own compilation needs it, has a rerun-if-changed directive
exports a data_dir() function analogous to config_dir(), with a unit test asserting the XDG_DATA_HOME-unset default path

key_links: src/main.rs's SqliteStore::open call uses herdctl::config::data_dir(), not config.static_dir
prohibitions: No change to the sqlite schema or Store trait

trace.outcome: Added rust-embed+axum-embed deps, a build.rs guard that create_dir_all(static) so a fresh checkout compiles without npm run bundle, a new config::data_dir() (XDG_DATA_HOME-aware, byte-identical to install.sh's default SHARE_DIR) with a unit test, and decoupled the sqlite state path in main.rs from config.static_dir to data_dir().
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["src/config/mod.rs (existing config_dir/ensure_config/ensure_web_secret tests)", "src/main.rs (no existing unit tests; store wiring is integration-level)"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": ["src/config/mod.rs: data_dir_defaults_to_home_local_share (new) — asserts XDG_DATA_HOME-unset default resolves to $HOME/.local/share/herdr-gateway, matching install.sh's SHARE_DIR"],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Prior behavior (git show HEAD:src/main.rs:173-183): sqlite path was derived as config.static_dir.parent().unwrap_or(\".\").join(\"herdctl-state.sqlite\") — only correct because install.sh happened to write static_dir as $SHARE_DIR/static. This coupling is real but not a failing test; it's a latent bug the plan/approach docs identified (approach.md: 'Real coupling found, not anticipated in the original ask'). No pre-existing test exercised the old derivation, so there is no red-to-green transition to show beyond the new data_dir() unit test proving the replacement path is correct.",
  "verification_run": "cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings -> 84 tests passed (incl. new data_dir_defaults_to_home_local_share), 3 additional tests passed, 0 failed, clippy clean with -D warnings. Additionally confirmed fresh-clone path: rm -rf static && cargo test --quiet -> build.rs recreated static/ via create_dir_all, all 84 tests still passed.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["No red-state test exists for the old static_dir-derived path since it was never covered by a unit test; characterized via git show + approach.md discovery notes instead of a failing-test artifact."]
}
trace.verify_output: cargo test: 84 passed, 3 passed (doc-adjacent), 0 failed, 0 ignored across all targets. cargo clippy -D warnings: clean, no warnings/errors. Also confirmed: removing static/ and rerunning cargo test still passes 84/84 (build.rs recreates static/ via create_dir_all before RustEmbed derive would scan it).
